cb_step_1.title: Select the CT volume from the scene
cb_step_1.instruction: Select the CT volume from the scene.

cb_step_5.title: Set the threshold range for bone segmentation

cb_step_8.title: Select the 'Split islands to segments' option

cb_step_10.title: Select the fixed reference segmentation node
cb_step_10.instruction: Select the reference segment from the list.

cb_step_12.title: Select the moving segmentation node
cb_step_12.instruction: Select the moving segment from the list.

cb_step_17.title: Adjust the 3D view
cb_step_17.instruction: Rotate and zoom to get a clear view of the moving fragment.

cb_step_18.title: Click the 'Harden transform into moving fragment' button

operation_descriptions: Switch to Segment Editor module and create a new segmentation named 'bone_segmentation'
Add a new segment named 'bone_segment' inside the segmentation
Activate the Threshold effect in Segment Editor
Apply the Threshold effect
Activate the Islands effect in Segment Editor
Select the 'Split islands to segments' option within Islands effect
Apply the Islands effect
Switch to Segment Editor module and create a new segmentation named 'bone_segmentation'
Add a new segment named 'bone_segment' inside the segmentation
Activate the Threshold effect in Segment Editor
Apply the Threshold effect
Activate the Islands effect in Segment Editor
Select the 'Split islands to segments' option within Islands effect
Apply the Islands effect
Reconstruct 3D model from the reference segmentation
Reconstruct 3D model from the moving segmentation
Detect fracture surfaces from the reference and moving model
Plan fracture reduction by running registration and creating output transform
Manually adjust the moving fragment.
Harden the transform into the moving fragment model

ground_api_evidence_note: ground_api_evidence is pre-revision retrieval evidence; final_api_evidence reflects the current validated templates.
